item.title: Gari Yala report finds racism against First Nations workers widespread
item.description: A landmark Indigenous-led report reveals that racism remains a daily reality for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander workers and, at the current rate of change, "it will take 118 years to eliminate racial slurs from Australian workplaces".

item.title: Will MotoGP in the Adelaide parklands reignite the land grab debate this election?
item.description: With the announcements of MotoGP and LIV Golf taking up public green spaces, environment has become a key concern raised by South Australian voters to ABC's Your Say.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Travel quiz, March 11: Can you find your way around the world?
item.description: Where would you find the Frank Lloyd Wright marvel, Fallingwater? And what's the capital of Fiji?

item.title: Cannot find Greek yoghurt in the supermarket aisle? You are not alone
item.description: Greek yoghurt has been flying off the shelves as viral social media trends spruik its high-protein benefits.

item.title: Nationals face leadership contest after David Littleproud steps down
item.description: Four Nationals are emerging as contenders to lead the party, with Bridget McKenzie, Matt Canavan and Kevin Hogan declaring their candidacy.

item.title: Bundaberg flooding
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Search for two international tourists missing in widespread flooding
item.description: A car belonging to two international tourists who failed to arrive at their destination has been found.

item.title: Foreign Facebook accounts using AI Pauline Hanson to manipulate Australians
item.description: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation uncovers a raft of foreign Facebook accounts targeting Australian users. And they're just the tip of the iceberg.

item.title: Former Don Dale Youth Detention Centre inmates take appeal over compensation for tear gas incident to High Court
item.description: A challenge to a ruling curbing access to compensation for four former inmates of Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were sprayed with CS gas in 2014 will be heard at the High Court in Canberra today.

item.title: Bill Kerr was the man world leaders trusted to keep them safe in Australia
item.description: The family of a former NSW Police detective says while his job was to ensure the safety of foreign leaders, at home he was just Dad.

item.title: Supporters gather at Sydney airport ahead of Iranian footballers' outbound flight
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia closes several Middle East diplomatic missions, Wong confirms — as it happened
item.description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong has confirmed Australia has closed several diplomatic missions in the Middle East amid the ongoing war.

item.title: Women with Asherman's syndrome call for tighter control of outdated practices
item.description: Outdated medical devices are being used to perform a common obstetrics procedure on Australian women that can cause a condition known as Asherman's syndrome.

item.title: Bundaberg residents told not to return home yet after flood watch downgraded - as it happened
item.description: Bundaberg is facing its worst flood in more than a decade, with the Burnett River peaking at about 7.4 metres.

item.title: Australian Medical Association speaks out after top doctor suspended from Albury Wodonga Health
item.description: The Australian Medical Association is reviewing why several top doctors and nurses have left Albury Wodonga hospital.

item.title: South Australian Dan Turner completes dream of sailing around the world in homemade boat
item.description: After more than a year of sailing and thousands of nautical miles covered, Dan Turner completes a race around the globe in the boat he built in his driveway.

item.title: Woman who lost life savings in phishing scam takes on ING Bank, and wins
item.description: Claudia Lee's ING savings account was drained by a scammer while she was travelling overseas — but it took 18 months for the bank to be forced to take responsibility.

item.title: ASX posts modest win as Asian markets regain ground — as it happened
item.description: A positive day on the ASX 200 as Australia's market posts gains despite Westpac and the National Australia Bank predicting the RBA will lift rates by 25 basis points in March and May.

item.title: Family members of people killed in e-rideable accidents left disappointed by lack of urgency from authorities
item.description: The families of two West Australians killed in e-scooter accidents say they are disappointed the state government is not acting sooner to better regulate e-rideable devices.

item.title: UEFA Champions League: Spurs keeper hooked after 17 minutes against Atletico, Liverpool upset by Galatasaray, Yamal penalty denies Newcastle
item.description: Tottenham's nightmare season drops to an embarrassing new low as Antonin Kinsky is marched off the field by his manager in Madrid.

item.title: Bundaberg Flooding drone footage
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Member of Iranian football team granted asylum requests return to Iran
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says one of the seven members of the Iranian women's football delegation granted asylum in Australia has changed their mind and wants to return to Iran. Iranian embassy officials have collected the woman from the hotel where she was under police protection.

item.title: Australian designer Katie Perry wins trademark case against popstar Katy Perry in High Court
item.description: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry wins her High Court battle against international pop star Katy Perry in a long-running trademark case.

item.title: Indigenous Dance College NAISDA celebrates 50 years of cultural pride
item.description: Australia's longest-running First Nations dance academy has helped to shape the cultural fabric of the nation.

item.title: There is a constant reminder in Roebourne of a massacre 160 years ago
item.description: In the Pilbara town of Roebourne the name of many streets haunt residents as they are named after perpetrators of a massacre which decimated the Yaburara tribe 160 years ago.

item.title: Major floods threaten NT remote communities of Daly River and Beswick, as return to school begins in Katherine
item.description: Schools around Katherine are starting to reopen in the wake of flooding over the weekend, though major flooding is continuing to threaten a remote Northern Territory community nearby.

item.title: SA Liberal Party candidate says 'same-sex marriage is not real' and 'feminism is demonic'
item.description: A Liberal candidate says "same-sex marriage is not real" and "feminism is demonic", in views Liberal leader Ashton Hurn says she does not share, but will leave up to voters to decide. Warning: This story contains content some readers might find distressing.

item.title: Indian Wells 2026: Talia Gibson scores upset win over Jasmine Paolini, Aryna Sabalenka outduels Naomi Osaka
item.description: Talia Gibson scores one of the biggest wins of her young career as she advances at Indian Wells, while Aryna Sabalenka shows off one of the newest members of her team.

item.title: Matt Canavan vows to 'fight back' as he rises to the Nationals leadership
item.description: With a populist economic message, the new Nationals leader said local manufacturing and higher birth rates were key as he rejected the labels of "left" and "right" politics.

item.title: St Basil's aged care home in Fawkner fined $150k over breaches relating to fatal COVID outbreak
item.description: St Basil's aged care in Fawkner has been fined $150,000 for failing to provide adequate training to staff in 2020.

item.title: Craig Freeman pleads not guilty to 1991 murder of wife Melinda
item.description: Mr Freeman was charged last year for the murder of his then-wife, who was found dead in their home in Melbourne's north 30 years ago.

item.title: Brisbane Roar offers training and support to Iranian asylum seeker players
item.description: A-League clubs reach out to the Iranian women's players granted asylum, offering training, support and a new "home".

item.title: Australia's pesticide regulator sets new rules banning some rat baits from public sale
item.description: Rat baits could soon be restricted to licensed operators after a change in regulations from the Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority.

item.title: Doctor expresses concern for memory of driver who crashed into group of people in Daylesford
item.description: The diabetes doctor of the driver responsible for a 2023 collision in the Victorian town of Daylesford, that killed five people, tells a coronial inquest that he is "concerned" for his patient's memory.

item.title: Australian Federal Police officer pleads guilty to making false sworn legal statements before trial
item.description: Detective Leading Senior Constable Trent Madders was due to face trial next week, accused of perjury, concealing evidence and perverting the course of justice, related to incidents in June 2019 and August 2020.

item.title: Why Iran is targeting UAE more than other Gulf states in war with US and Israel
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is reporting more Iranian attacks than any other country as Tehran continues to retaliate against US and Israeli strikes.

item.title: Adelaide criminal defence lawyer Andrew Graham vows to contest rape charge
item.description: Adelaide criminal defence lawyer Andrew Robert Graham says he will fight a charge of rape, saying the allegation "is untrue".

item.title: Corio man charged over triple-fatal crash on Western Highway at Armstrong near Ararat
item.description: Two Victorians and a South Australian man died in the crash involving five vehicles on the Western Highway near Ararat.

item.title: Tony Popovic turns attention to finer details as Socceroos close in on 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic is known for his attention to detail and is leaving no stone unturned as he prepares his squad for the World Cup.

item.title: Farmers devastated by NT floods feel overlooked by emergency response
item.description: NT farmers counting the cost of livestock deaths and crop damage say they feel overlooked by the emergency flood response.

item.title: WOMADelaide 2026 review: How a city found joy in a global music celebration
item.description: At Adelaide's global music weekender, tens of thousands transform everyday anxieties into a shared burst of joy.

item.title: Anonymous gamblers betting on Iran strikes make millions on Polymarket
item.description: Gambling experts and US lawmakers have raised concerns about the ethics of online betting and whether insider information is helping profiteers cash in on war.

item.title: Cody Schaeffer, former youth advocate, pleads guilty to assaulting five teenagers
item.description: The court heard Cody Schaeffer, who founded Borderline Australia, paid each boy to be "hogtied" and "have their feet slapped with coat hangers".

item.title: Darwin River residents say they have 'lost everything' in flooding that struck without warning
item.description: People have had to be rescued from croc-inhabited waters and some have lost everything following flooding in Darwin River.

item.title: Man accused of murdering mother in Gunnedah caravan park to face Supreme Court trial
item.description: Callan Davidson, charged with domestic violence-related murder after the body of his mother Cheryl Davidson was found at a caravan park, has pleaded not guilty before local court today.

item.title: Perth Festival 2026: Our favourites from Haribo Kimchi to Ngaiire
item.description: Operatic punk, a Korean snack stand on stage, and a return to the golden age of dance halls were some of the highlights of the festival in 2026.

item.title: Fuel shortages hit rural Queensland businesses
item.description: Growing fuel uncertainty is hitting rural and fisheries businesses across regional Queensland, potentially costing them thousands in lost production and higher operating costs.

item.title: Adelaide man Craig Sansbury-Weetra avoids jail for 'cowardly act' during CBD attack
item.description: An Adelaide man will serve a home detention sentence for an attack in the city's nightlife precinct that left his "off-guard" victim learning how to walk and eat independently again.

item.title: Linq Buslines and two directors plead guilty to safety breaches after deadly crash
item.description: The bus company linked to the deadly 2023 Hunter Valley wedding bus crash and a director plead guilty to heavy-vehicle law safety breaches.

item.title: Government doubles graduate visa fee but Pacific Island countries exempt
item.description: Tiered pricing for visas is being used to support Pacific nations but some fear it could be misused to target certain communities.

item.title: Sarah Game tells voters not to put her party first after split with Chris McDermott
item.description: A South Australian politician is urging voters to not back her own party in the upper house after a dramatic breakdown with star candidate and former Adelaide Crows captain Chris McDermott.

item.title: Doongmabulla Springs case proceeds to civil trial in Queensland's Supreme Court
item.description: The dispute centres on the Doongmbulla Springs, near the Carmichael River in central Queensland, with traditional custodians accusing the Queensland government of failing to protect the sacred site.

item.title: Boil water alert remains for Darwin area after flood takes out dam pump
item.description: People in Darwin, Palmerstorn and its surrounding areas will need to keep boiling water before consumption for the foreseeable future, as work to restore the Darwin River Dam's pumps continue.

item.title: Nicholas James Alexander jailed over antisemitic attacks across Sydney
item.description: Nicholas James Alexander was jailed for a maximum of five years for his role orchestrating a series of antisemitic attacks across Sydney, including the fire-bombing of a childcare centre.

item.title: Matt Canavan is National Party's unusual leader with dreams of fossil-fuel powered future
item.description: Matt Canavan once claimed to be an atypical Nationals member. Now the pro-mining Queenslander is leading the party from the Senate.

item.title: Assessing the flood damage at Darwin River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aerial footage of Darwin River floods
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women tell court Barry Cable sexually assaulted them as children in Melbourne backyard spas
item.description: Former footballer Barry Cable sexually assaulted children in a backyard spa in Melbourne at a time when he was a "famous" figure, a court has heard.

item.title: The Tasmania Devils won't play AFL at Macquarie Point earlier than 2031. Should we be surprised?
item.description: Tasmania's future AFL club believed they would be playing in their new $1.13-billion stadium from 2030, but it's now clear their debut there will be at least one year later.

item.title: Economists scramble to change their interest rate forecasts after RBA speaks
item.description: Expectations the Reserve Bank will hike the cash rate next week are rising as leading economists shift their forecasts — and some say one increase may not be enough.

item.title: Mother of Iranian women's footballer pleads with player to stay in Australia as families face threats
item.description: The smiles and joy seen in pictures of the seven members of the Iranian women's football team who sought asylum in Australia only tell part of the story, with many still scared for their families back home.

item.title: More views of SA Liberal candidate emerge saying Islam 'poisonous, wrong', 'trans agenda is insanity'
item.description: More views by Liberal Party candidate and evangelical Christian Carston Woodhouse emerge with the Adelaide man saying Islam is "poisonous and wrong" and the "trans agenda is insanity". Warning: This story contains content some readers might find distressing.

item.title: How the Middle East war and the cost of fuel is sending airfares skyward
item.description: As Qantas hikes international airfares due to a surge in fuel prices from the Middle East war, an industry expert warns it's only the start of what's to come.

item.title: Flower importers feel the pinch as Iran war affects flight routes
item.description: The war in the Middle East is not just affecting petrol prices and travel plans — florists and their suppliers have suddenly found themselves unable to supply blooms due to the disruption of air freight routes.

item.title: CLP knocks back bid to refer Tanzil Rahman bullying claims to parliamentary inquiry
item.description: A Northern Territory government backbencher accused of bullying has faced a bid for a parliamentary inquiry into his alleged conduct, as well as an extraordinary berating from the NT Speaker.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: WA government rules out petrol price cap as fuel costs soar amid Middle East war
item.description: The WA government has moved to allay concerns around fuel price and supply, after a roundtable meeting in Perth on Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Tasmania Police officer and a Franklin River blockade photographer reunite after four decades
item.description: Ian Wright captured the Franklin River blockade on film. Forty years later, the officer who arrested him reached out.

item.title: Bird deaths linked to fish lost in algal bloom, SA Research and Development Institute says
item.description: Birds are starving and dying along metropolitan Adelaide beaches, caused by a lack of fish as a result of the algal bloom, the South Australian government says.

item.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson was originally tapped by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in the weeks after the Bondi terror attack to lead a review into the federal government's intelligence and security agencies.

item.title: Matt Canavan may blunt One Nation but can he help rebuild the Coalition?
item.description: Matt Canavan's rise to the Nationals leadership gives the Coalition a fighter capable of taking on Pauline Hanson, but beating back One Nation is only part of the opposition's electoral dilemma.

item.title: Victorian government vows to shift building inspection costs from buyers to sellers
item.description: The Victorian government says it will make home sellers pay for building and pest inspections rather than home buyers if it wins this year's state election in November.

item.title: The real-life story behind The Voice of Hind Rajab
item.description: Protests erupted following the killing of six-year-old Hind Rajab at the hands of Israeli forces. Two years later, her story is in the running for an Academy Award.

item.title: Victorian wineries sell vineyards to gold exploration company amid global headwinds
item.description: As the wine industry struggles with increased challenges, some Victorian wineries are choosing to sell their land to mining companies.

item.title: Approval granted for major expansion of Porters Bridge Road Quarry despite concerns
item.description: Locals say the "continuous rumbling" from a quarry in Tasmania's Meander Valley is a major disturbance. The company which owns it has just been given approval to increase production by 500 per cent.

item.title: Record 400 million barrels of oil to be released from reserves to stall price hike
item.description: The International Energy Agency agrees to release 400 million barrels of oil, the largest such move in its history. Australia says it is considering the decision, which would not involve sending oil overseas.

item.title: Iconic Australian-made pinball machines preserved for generations to come
item.description: Five pinball machines, including the first licensed Star Wars unit, have been saved from the scrap heap and will eventually be made available for the public to play at a special exhibition.

item.title: Kalyn Ponga makes international rugby league switch to represent New Zealand
item.description: Kalyn Ponga says previous appearances for Australia did not reflect his true national identity after the International Rugby League approved his application to switch allegiances to New Zealand.

item.title: Korean-born Australian woman overturns adoption as federal inquiry launched
item.description: A Korean-born Australian who was sexually assaulted growing up is calling for changes to adoption laws after struggling to remove her adoptive parents from her birth certificate.

item.title: Alleged carjacker arrested and charged over Seabrook crash
item.description: A 19-year-old man will face court after walking away from a crash which tore a carjacked vehicle in half in Melbourne's south-west last week.

item.title: Adelaide Hills residents living near fertiliser firm Neutrog want answers on air quality
item.description: Residents in the Adelaide Hills town of Kanmantoo and surrounds have been dealing with a growing regularity of pungent odour but the fertiliser company, who pleaded guilty to environmental breaches, says its chicken litter presents no risk.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and is providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: ABC Oscars quiz: How well do you remember the winners and losers from the past 10 years?
item.description: Test your knowledge of who went home with a statue and who was honoured just to be nominated.

item.title: Sydney's abnormal heat, humidity levels due to warm Tasman Sea and northerly winds
item.description: Sydney's humidity has been rivalling the tropics in the middle of wet season, with the stifling conditions expected to linger into autumn.

item.title: Chinchilla residents told not to return to flood-affected areas as Bundaberg counts cost
item.description: Residents in Chinchilla are being told not to return to some areas as major flooding continues.

item.title: Victorian Farmers Federation president Brett Hosking announces shock exit
item.description: Victorian Farmers Federation president Brett Hosking resigns amid rumours he is seeking political pre-selection.

item.title: Bowen says Middle East conflict a 'crisis' for fuel supply — as it happened
item.description: Energy Minister Chris Bowen has told Question Time that the Middle East conflict is presenting a fuel supply "crisis".

item.title: Shoalhaven residents to choose between military helicopters to replace 'chopper on a stick' landmark
item.description: Residents on the NSW South Coast are being asked to decide which model helicopter they want to replace Nowra's "chopper on a stick" landmark.

item.title: North Queensland residents say electoral boundary changes leave them 'short-changed'
item.description: With one parliamentarian from the far north set to go, residents in some areas will go from being 15 minutes' drive away from their local MP to 15 hours.

item.title: More than 100 doctors file no-confidence vote in regional health service executive management
item.description: Two top doctors and a nurse have left Albury Wodonga hospital in just six months, prompting a vote of no confidence in management.

item.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
item.description: Some advocates say vitamin IV drips can promote clearer thinking, immunity, beauty and even anti-aging, but health experts say there is little research to back the claims.

item.title: Iran in doubt to play men's FIFA World Cup following US air strikes
item.description: Iran's sports minister says his country cannot compete in the men's FIFA World Cup, which is co-hosted by the United States.

item.title: Over 3 million Australians experienced personal fraud last financial year — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 has fallen sharply as the oil price skyrockets in Asian trade, and a planned major release of crude supplies by the IEA has failed to calm markets.

item.title: Jason Bennett returns to VFL coverage with the ABC after television and radio deal struck
item.description: Respected VFL commentator Jason Bennett returns to the coverage in 2026 after the ABC picked up the broadcast rights to the league.

item.title: Inside the vibrant world of brass and concert bands in Australia
item.description: From gold rush towns to Mardi Gras parades, brass and concert bands have been the musical backbone of Australian communities over a century.

item.title: After 40 years of parenthood incentives Singapore's fertility rate is still falling
item.description: Singapore is well-known for its social engineering to manage population growth. So why has the island city recorded its lowest-ever fertility rate again?

item.title: Australia's summers are getting longer with more extreme heat
item.description: Australia has always been hot. On average, summer is now one degree warmer than it was 50 years ago. But that number hides the stark reality that the summers our kids experience today are much more extreme and longer than they used to be.

item.title: WA pensioner Bill Turner chooses to 'live for today' as state's rental crisis hits hard
item.description: At 76 and stuck on the waitlist for public housing, Bill Turner is on the brink of homelessness, unable to afford his own rental in a housing market at breaking point — a situation that's left him feeling "deserted".

item.title: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he was 'surplus to requirements' of royal commission
item.description: Former ASIO boss Dennis Richardson says he resigned from the royal commission into antisemitism because he felt he was overpaid and "surplus to requirements".

item.title: After watching all 211 of Alex Johnston's NRL tries, these five are the most important
item.description: After scouring the internet for footage of all 211 tries Alex Johnston has scored during his NRL career, these five are the most important to his journey towards the all-time record.

item.title: Targeting mistake led to US missile strike on Iranian girls school: media reports
item.description: A US military investigation has determined that a US Tomahawk missile struck an Iranian elementary school because of a targeting mistake, The New York Times has reported.

item.title: Cambodia says it will shut down online scam centres by the end of April
item.description: Cambodian authorities say they have shut down 80 per cent of locations believed to be carrying out online scams. But some are sceptical given "key perpetrators" among the country's ruling elite.

item.title: Bodies found in search for two Chinese backpackers missing in floodwater at Kilkivan, Queensland
item.description: The pair were reported missing when they failed to reach their destination while driving north on Tuesday, with police confirming two bodies were found inside their car.

item.title: Islamic Society criticises 'competition' to attack Muslims as SA Liberals stand by candidate
item.description: An organisation representing South Australian Muslims criticises the Liberal Party and One Nation for what it says is a rush to attack Islam "more harshly in order to gain political support" ahead of the state election.

item.title: NSW defeat Tasmania by four wickets to win One Day Cup final
item.description: A steady hand from skipper Kurtis Patterson, after early damage by Sean Abbott and Ben Dwarshuis, has helped NSW to a One Day Cup title with a rain-hit four-wicket win over Tasmania.

item.title: Teenage boy killed and two others injured in crash west of Brisbane
item.description: Emergency services were called to the scene at Bertha Street and Brisbane Road in the Ipswich suburb Goodna about 9pm last night.

item.title: Australian sports presenter Mel McLaughlin reveals lung cancer diagnosis
item.description: Australian sports presenter Mel McLaughlin says she has had treatment for lung cancer, after being absent from TV screens since January.

item.title: Video appears to show US Tomahawk hitting near Minab girls' school, experts say
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland aviation operator accused of organising people-smuggling 'black flight'
item.description: The Australian Federal Police was alerted to the alleged people-smuggling operation when two wanted Sydney men landed in Indonesia in a light aircraft.

item.title: NT flood risk focus shifts to Victoria River as tropical low moves south-west
item.description: The tropical low that has brought flooding across large parts of the Top End for the past week is moving south-west, with residents near Victoria River warned to brace for heavy rainfall in coming days.

item.title: Helicopter rescue of a German Shepherd west of Katherine
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Beth Mooney sold for record fee in Women's Hundred auction
item.description: Australia's evergreen run machine Beth Mooney has cashed in on her enduring excellence, scooping a joint-record payday of $395,000 in the auction for The Women's Hundred.

item.title: Mother jailed for criminal neglect of three children, including 6yo Charlie, who died
item.description: A mother who admitted to criminally neglecting three of her children, including her daughter who died when she was six-years-old, has been jailed for 17 years.

item.title: Country councils putting compulsory voting for WA's local government elections back on agenda
item.description: Councillors in one of WA's biggest regional cities back making voting at local government elections compulsory, amid growing concerns about low turnout and fringe candidates running for councils across the state.

item.title: Atlassian cuts 1,600 jobs from global workforce as AI slashes labour needs
item.description: Atlassian's Australian workforce will account for 30 per cent of the tech giant's global job cuts, with the freed-up funds to be invested in AI and enterprise sales.

item.title: Deeper oil crisis could add full percentage point to inflation, Treasury warns
item.description: Treasury has advised the government that if the oil price averages $US100 per barrel for three months, there would be a temporary 0.5 percentage point spike to headline inflation.

item.title: How can you be a more responsible dog owner? Readers gave us their dos and don'ts
item.description: Here's how you can become a more responsible dog owner — according to your fellow readers.

item.title: Britain and the US had a special bond. This war might have changed that
item.description: Many nations, including Australia, claim to have a special relationship with the United States. Britain, and some in Europe, are finding out that's no longer the case.

item.title: Former Department of Communities employee Barry Wayne Lawrence facing child sex abuse charges
item.description: A West Australian man honoured with a prestigious award for excellence in child protection has been charged with raping a teenage boy.

item.title: How Murder on the Dancefloor led Sophie Ellis-Bextor to a Perimenopop disco party
item.description: Megahits Murder on the Dancefloor and Groovejet made her a household name but Sophie Ellis-Bextor is so much more.

item.title: Boil water alert lifted in Darwin after testing shows region's water is free of E.coli and other 'harmful bacteria'
item.description: The boil water alert for Darwin has been lifted after testing confirmed the region's water was free of E.coli and other harmful bacteria.

item.title: Katie Perry wins High Court trademark case against Katy Perry
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA Liberal candidate no longer running for party after podcast comments
item.description: SA Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn says one of her candidates for the upcoming state election is no longer running for the party after comments he made on Islam, feminism, same-sex marriage and "the trans agenda" came to light from an American podcast.

item.title: Oil price breaks the $US100 per barrel mark as oil tankers are hit in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The oil price has surged again amid Iranian strikes on oil tankers and the fear there could be more attacks to come.

item.title: Fuel standards relaxed to add 100 million litres of fuel to Australian supply
item.description: Australia will bring in 100 million more litres of petrol by allowing a higher level of sulfur content in its fuel for the next 60 days, Energy Minister Chris Bowen says, as the country reckons with petrol flows choked by the Middle East war.

item.title: Tigers hopeful Heamasi Makasini stays with NRL club amid talk of rugby union switch
item.description: Heamasi Makasini has shown off his talent in both rugby codes, but Wests Tigers are hopeful they will keep the 18-year-old at the club once his contract expires.

item.title: Katherine flood victims forced to stand in line for hours for relief payments
item.description: Katherine region residents displaced from their homes by the recent flood emergency have been forced to wait in line for hours to access disaster relief payments.

item.title: Wife of Australian footballer Barry Cable takes stand in his child sex abuse trial
item.description: The wife of Barry Cable, the former footballer on trial for allegedly sexually abusing a child in the early stages of his illustrious career, testifies in defence of her famous husband.

item.title: Albanese government won't say the 'w' word about Iran
item.description: This week in Canberra the Nationals chose a new leader, the energy minister chose his words carefully and the government chose to avoid one word altogether.

item.title: Novak Djokovic loses to Jack Draper in three sets at Indian Wells tournament
item.description: World number three Novak Djokovic falls to defending champion Jack Draper in a pulsating fourth-round match at the prestigious Indian Wells tournament.

item.title: Tahmoor Colliery liquidators confirm up to 238 redundancies
item.description: Up to 238 workers at Tahmoor Colliery in south-west Sydney are being made redundant as liquidators attempt to sell the coal mine, following months of financial turmoil under Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance.

item.title: Disaster assistance payments have been expanded in the Northern Territory. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Residents in more flood-affected communities across the Top End are now eligible for hardship payments, with disaster assistance also extended further in the Barkly and Central Australia.

item.title: Coral Adventurer grounding preliminary investigation
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Roosters NRL player Billy Smith involved in e-bike accident ahead of Rabbitohs clash
item.description: Sydney Roosters centre Billy Smith will miss Friday night's match against the Rabbitohs after he was involved in an e-bike accident near the club's headquarters.

item.title: 'Black rain' in Tehran following Israeli strikes on oil depots
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man who killed Perth music promoter Andrew Scally in suburban street sentenced to jail
item.description: Jardyn Rigo is sentenced to more than six years in jail for killing music promoter Andrew Scally in a street fight.

item.title: Parents and disability advocates shocked as specialist service AEIOU closes with no notice
item.description: In a statement published on Wednesday, early intervention care provider AEIOU announced the board had appointed external administrators to the organisation.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Raso, Catley ruled out, with Matildas prepared for anything against North Korea
item.description: Hayley Raso and Steph Catley will miss the Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal, as the team prepares for the unknown quantities that North Korea may bring.

item.title: Blues hang on for scratchy but vital win over Tigers at the MCG
item.description: Carlton kicks only one goal in the second half, but it is just enough to hold off a late charge by a wayward Richmond at the MCG.

item.title: Hobart Clinic chief resigns months after appointment amid reported clash with board
item.description: The Hobart Clinic's chief executive has resigned as the state government commissions a review of the mental health hospital's corporate governance.

item.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: 'Absolute madness': Broncos remain winless after Eels win bizarre NRL clash
item.description: The Broncos' defence is labelled "pathetic" as they surrender a 20-6 lead, hit back in the second half but eventually lose 40-32 on the back of future teammate Jonah Pezet's game-sealing try.

item.title: Queensland coroner to probe whether death of eight-year-old Elizabeth Struhs was preventable
item.description: The Queensland coroner will examine interactions the Struhs family had with government agencies before the eight-year-old's death at the family's Toowoomba home in 2022.

item.title: Co-founder of Copenhagen's Noma steps down after abuse allegations
item.description: René Redzepi, co-founder of a restaurant repeatedly named the world's best, has announced he is stepping down after reports was abusive to staff at the famed Copenhagen restaurant.

item.title: Drought-hit WA pastoralists plead for funds to move hay stored 1,000km away
item.description: After years of drought, pastoralists in WA are desperate for fodder for their cattle. A charity has a supply in storage for them — but no money for fuel to transport it.

item.title: ADF member facing 'significant' assault charges against colleague granted bail
item.description: A Darwin-based soldier accused of assaulting and robbing a fellow service person has been bailed to travel home to NSW.

item.title: Firefighters battle burning fuel tanks in video released by Bahrain government
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oil tankers burn as Iran's new leader vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
item.description: Iran's new supreme leader says the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed as a tool of pressure, vowing to "obtain compensation from the enemy".

item.title: Israel's military drops prosecution of reservists who allegedly raped Palestinian detainee
item.description: Israel's military drops the prosecution of five reservists accused of the violent rape of a Palestinian man at the Sde Teiman prison, under the cover of the war with Iran.

item.title: Attacks on veteran MP force Victorian Liberal party to reissue campaign material
item.description: Liberal party MPs and insiders have been infuriated by the publishing of a preselection candidate's blistering statement about a veteran MP.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs North Korea
item.description: The knockout stage of the Women's Asian Cup is here, and tonight the Matildas will face the might of North Korea in their quarter-final in Perth.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The full-throttle, mysterious North Korean side looking to rain on the Matildas' parade
item.description: Sensational protests, head-scratching substitutions and some seriously impressive play — North Korea is one of Asian Cup's best sides, and certainly its most mysterious. Here is what the Matildas can expect in their blockbuster quarterfinal tonight in Perth.

item.title: Hugo Weaving talks about loving art, hating learning lines, and what's next
item.description: Hugo Weaving may be an internationally renowned actor of both stage and screen, but he says he never feels ready for his next big role.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A new Nationals leader, a curious crocodile and the duelling Perrys
item.description: Think you've been paying attention to the news cycle this week? Test your memory here.

item.title: Pokémon’s virtual citizen science taught Australian entomology how to catch ‘em all
item.description: As Pokémon turns 30, a video game inspired by insect-collecting continues to shape how scientists think and teach about bugs today.

item.title: What should you do with the family home after a divorce?
item.description: For many Australians, the decision to divorce has become even more complicated because of the rising cost of housing. Some are now calling for policy changes to make it easier for single parents to re-enter the housing market.

item.title: On the Iraq-Iran border Kurdish troops are gathering to fight their own battle against the Khamenei regime
item.description: On Iraq's border with Iran, troops are amassing as Iranian Kurds from across the globe gather to revolt after 47 years of being the victims of an oppressive and cruel regime.

item.title: MP whose electorate gets largest capital gains tax discount wants to rein it in
item.description: New analysis shows five of the six electorates with the largest capital gains tax discount benefits are held by independents.

item.title: Dennis Richardson's exit puts antisemitism royal commissioner under more pressure
item.description: As soon as the royal commission was announced, Dennis Richardson must have known his situation was going to be tricky.

item.title: Iranian women's team not the first athletes to seek asylum while competing overseas
item.description: The choice for some Iranian women's soccer team members to stay in Australia is not the first time athletes have travelled for a sporting competition and chosen to stay.

item.title: 'An old fat lazy cat': Estonia says Putin and Trump gave Europe a wake-up call
item.description: Estonia's Foreign Minister has declared that Europe was like an "old fat lazy cat" in the aftermath of the Cold War and has vowed that NATO forces will strike "into the heart of Russia" if Moscow invades Baltic countries.

item.title: Fears grow for Iranian footballers as activists mount last-ditch rescue bid from Malaysia
item.description: Iranian activists are racing against time to convince members of the country's female football team to turn back from Malaysia, as fears grow that the women are being coerced home by regime officials.

item.title: Stakeholders race to find Liberty Bell Bay smelter buyer as job losses loom
item.description: A northern Tasmanian business group wants to secure a buyer for Australia's only manganese alloy smelter as the corporate regulator threatens its current owners with liquidation.

item.title: How Donald Trump and JD Vance are 'philosophically a little bit different' on Iran
item.description: From MAGA-loathing "hillbilly" to vice-president, JD Vance's rise and rebirth has been something to behold. But the war in Iran may test his considerable political dexterity.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas apologises for failing to deliver on his promise to 'fix the ramping crisis'
item.description: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas says there is "no shirking the fact" his government has failed to deliver on its key election promise to fix the ramping crisis — a failure for which he has apologised.

item.title: Liana France 15th in Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics Games women's giant slalom standing
item.description: Flying a pink Australian flag with her face on it, para-alpine skier Liana France has marked herself as one to watch at Paralympics to come.

item.title: Latrell Mitchell breaks his long-held silence to talk about the Winmarra foundation
item.description: NRL superstar Latrell Mitchell opens up about the Winmarra foundation, which he co-founded to empower emerging Indigenous leaders.

item.title: Report shows Albury Wodonga Health controversy fuelled staff 'distrust'
item.description: A report reveals a regional health service ordered a review into some of its departments over concerns a development controversy led to poor morale.

item.title: Family of the other Stephen King speaks amid SA government's email blunder
item.description: The family of a man who the state government mistook for someone else, igniting a pre-election debacle, has spoken for the first time.

item.title: Sydney mum facing living in her car as delays to NSW portable bond scheme drag on
item.description: After covering rent and bills, Jessica is left with just $15 to put into her savings, making it impossible for her to come up with the thousands of dollars needed for a new bond before her upcoming move.

item.title: Longreach prepares for flooding as Bundaberg clean-up begins
item.description: Hundreds of people in Queensland's Bundaberg region are cleaning up their homes and businesses as floodwaters recede, while residents in western Queensland prepare for flooding.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Disability community's access to sport to be tracked for first time in new Queensland project
item.description: Currently there is limited data across the country showing who in the disability community is participating in sport, who is missing out and why.

item.title: Benefits of Western Sydney International Airport not 'materialising' for locals
item.description: The communities closest to Western Sydney International (WSI) say having no direct public transport route proves they are living in the new airport's shadow.

item.title: Live: Netanyahu makes veiled threat against Iran's new supreme leader
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu holds his first press conference since the war began and gives a veiled threat to kill Iran's new supreme leader. Follow live.

item.title: Police and FBI at the scene of Michigan synagogue attack
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oscar Piastri moving on from Melbourne F1 crash and focused on Chinese Grand Prix
item.description: Just days removed from one of the most crushing moments in his Formula 1 career, Oscar Piastri says he is only focused on the future.

item.title: Hero swimmer Austin Appelbee given letter and gifts from Irish president Catherine Connolly
item.description: Perth schoolboy Austin Appelbee, who heroically swam for hours to save the lives of his mum and two siblings off the WA coast, is honoured with a note from Ireland's President Catherine Connolly.

item.title: Flood aftermath exposes holes in South Australia's federal road funding
item.description: As floodwater recedes and local governments begin calculating the damage to SA roads, they are frustrated it will be up to them to foot the bill in lieu of federal government support.

item.title: Live: Non-essential Australian officials directed to leave UAE, Israel
item.description: Non-essential Australian officials in the United Arab Emirates and Israel have been directed to leave as the security situation in the region deteriorates. Follow live.

item.title: Two teenagers die following e-bike and motorcycle crash south of Brisbane
item.description: Emergency services respond to a crash between an e-bike and motorcycle at Greenbank in Logan.

item.title: Broken Hill Council faces former legal provider in NSW Supreme Court over significant legal fees
item.description: A civil hearing between the Broken Hill City Council and its former legal provider over a major dispute in litigation fees has been heard in the Supreme Court of NSW.

item.title: The Matildas played North Korea in Pyongyang in 2007. It was unlike anything they'd ever experienced before
item.description: When the Matildas played North Korea in Pyongyang in 2007, it was like unlike anything they had experienced before.

item.title: Can you get the money out of politics?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Irymple grapevines inundated with water
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sunraysia and Murray Valley farmers seek disaster declaration after deluge during harvest
item.description: A deluge of rain at a critical stage of Sunraysia and Murray Valley's harvest and growing season leads farmers to call for it to be declared a disaster to unlock funds.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran is welcome in US for FIFA World Cup, but believes they should not play
item.description: US President Donald Trump says Iran men's national soccer team is welcome to participate in the 2026 World Cup, but believes it is not appropriate that they be there "for their own life and safety".

item.title: Union pushes for change to misconduct reporting in government-owned corporations
item.description: The Services Union says its members have complained about nepotism, favouritism in hiring and poor governance at a major Queensland ports corporation.

item.title: Judge hears closing arguments in Hobart Matriculation College abuse case
item.description: The lawyer representing an ex-teacher being sued for allegedly sexually abusing his former student in the 1980s tells a court that evidence given by the ex-student contained "straight-out lies".

item.title: Live: Oil hits $US100 again as Middle East supply shocks continue
item.description: Wall Street has traded sharply lower overnight as pessimism about the length of the war in the Middle East continues. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: A baby crocodile was spotted on this flooded road
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crocodiles can move into unexpected places like culverts during floods
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: A crocodile swimming near a footpath in Katherine
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crocodile next to flooded highway at Scotts Creek
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: A rescue on the flooded Donkey Flats Crossing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT authorities warn of crocodile risk as Top End flooding continues
item.description: Crocodiles have been spotted lurking in floodwaters across the Top End, with NT Police incident controller Shaun Gill warning the reptiles are "absolutely everywhere" right now.

item.title: Tensions grow between Water Corporation and mining giant Alcoa over water quality monitoring
item.description: Perth's water provider is growing increasingly frustrated with US mining giant Alcoa after facing roadblocks in accessing areas near the city's key drinking water source that it needs to keep the supply safe, internal documents reveal.

item.title: Teenage boy dies after allegedly stolen car crashes following carjacking attempt
item.description: Police say a teenage boy died when an allegedly stolen car crashed at Hoppers Crossing on Thursday night.

item.title: Fremantle's window wide open in AFL season 2026, but more pain in store for West Coast
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers and West Coast Eagles are starting the new AFL season at very different stages of the journey — and for one of them, the time to turn potential into performance and challenge for the premiership is now.
